commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: At the end of the third year, Daniel i. , and Jeremias xxv. Nabuchadonosor, in the first year of his reign, (Jeremias xxv. ) being associated in the empire by his aged father Nabopolassar, came up to attack Carchemis, (Jeremias xlvi. ) and the other dominions of Egypt, (ver. ) and their ally or vassal Joakim. He took the city of Jerusalem, and carried off many of the sacred vessels and captives, (Daniel i. ; Calmet) conducting the king himself to prison, for a short time, when he set him at liberty, on condition that he should pay tribute, 2 Paralipomenon xxxvi. (Tirinus) Joakim obeyed for 3 years. Hebrew, "he turned and rebelled. "This was the first time, as he had before been subject to Egypt, and was attacked on that account. He probably took advantage of the absence of Nabuchodonosor, who was gone with haste to secure all the dominions of his deceased father. In the 7th year of his reign, he sent rovers to punish Joakim. When the latter had reigned near 11 years, they reduced the kingdom, and treated the king's corpse with the utmost indignity, Jeremias xxii.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
